text: Great moments are born from great opportunity. And that's what you have here tonight, boys. That's what you've earned here tonight.
context: Setting the stage that this game is the moment they have worked for and earned.

text: One game. If we played 'em ten times, they might win nine. But not this game. Not tonight.
context: Acknowledging the odds while declaring that tonight the outcome will be different through will.

text: Tonight, we skate with them. Tonight, we stay with them. And we shut them down because we can!
context: Calling the team to match the Soviets stride for stride with belief in their own ability.

text: Tonight, we are the greatest hockey team in the world. You were born to be hockey players. Every one of you.
context: Affirming the team's destiny and that each player belongs on this historic stage.

text: This is your time. Their time is done. It's over.
context: The most quoted line — declaring the passing of the torch to this new generation.

text: I'm sick and tired of hearing about what a great hockey team the Soviets have. Screw 'em.
context: Dismissing the legend of the opponent to focus the team on their own power.

text: You don't have enough talent to win on talent alone.
context: The core philosophy that hard work, conditioning, and belief must overcome superior talent.

text: We should be dreaming. We grew up as kids having dreams, but now we're too sophisticated as adults. We stopped dreaming.
context: A call to reclaim childlike belief and ambition in the face of cynicism.
